prompt: I have a workspace with my-kafka (Kafka) and my-s3 (S3) connections. Create a processor that reads from Kafka topic 'logs', and writes all logs to S3 bucket 'data-lake' under the prefix 'raw/logs/' as JSON files.

assertions.name: uses_path_not_prefix
assertions.reason: Using 'prefix' instead of 'path' is the most common S3 sink mistake and causes processor failure

assertions.name: mentions_iam_prereq
assertions.description: Mentions IAM role ARN must be registered via Atlas Cloud Provider Access

assertions.name: valid_s3_emit_structure
assertions.description: $emit has connectionName, bucket, and path fields

common_mistakes.error: Processor fails with field validation error — 'prefix' is not a valid S3 $emit field
common_mistakes.fix: Use 'path' for the S3 destination path

common_mistakes.error: Processor may use default format or fail
$merge is for Atlas Cluster connections only
common_mistakes.fix: Use $emit for S3, Kafka, and Kinesis sinks

common_mistakes.mistake: Not mentioning IAM role ARN prerequisite
common_mistakes.error: S3 connection creation fails if IAM role is not registered via Cloud Provider Access
common_mistakes.fix: Always remind users to register IAM role ARN in Atlas before creating the S3 connection

skill_reference: SKILL.md: '$emit (S3): use path (NOT prefix). config.outputFormat required. IAM role ARN must be registered via Atlas Cloud Provider Access.'
